findings.title: Uncapped trade size from webhook payload
findings.description: quoteQty is extracted from webhook payload without a maximum cap. An attacker who compromises the webhook secret could submit a trade with quoteQty=999999, draining the Binance account.
findings.remediation: Add MAX_QUOTE_QTY config (e.g., 100 USDT) and clamp: quote_qty = min(float(quote_qty), config.MAX_QUOTE_QTY)

findings.title: Potential Telegram Bot Token in .env
findings.description: Detected what appears to be a Telegram Bot Token in configuration file.
findings.remediation: Move to a secrets manager or ensure this file is in .gitignore. Never commit real credentials to version control.

findings.title: Potential API key in .env
findings.description: Detected what appears to be a API key in configuration file.
findings.remediation: Move to a secrets manager or ensure this file is in .gitignore. Never commit real credentials to version control.

findings.title: Potential Telegram Bot Token in .env
findings.description: Detected what appears to be a Telegram Bot Token in configuration file.
findings.remediation: Move to a secrets manager or ensure this file is in .gitignore. Never commit real credentials to version control.

findings.title: Unsafe price/qty parsing without try/except
findings.description: float() called on user-controlled variable 'quote_qty' without try/except guard. A non-numeric webhook payload will crash the handler.
findings.remediation: Wrap in try/except (ValueError, TypeError) with safe default.

findings.title: Unsafe price/qty parsing without try/except
findings.description: float() called on user-controlled variable 'quote_qty' without try/except guard. A non-numeric webhook payload will crash the handler.
findings.remediation: Wrap in try/except (ValueError, TypeError) with safe default.

findings.title: Telegram bot token potentially exposed in error output
findings.description: Telegram bot token referenced in logging/error handling code. If the token value is interpolated into the message, it will appear in trades.log and could be exfiltrated.
findings.evidence: log.warning("TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not set — Telegram Bot disabled")
findings.remediation: Never log token values. Log only 'token_present=True/False'.

findings.title: Potential Hardcoded secret/password in simulate_webhook.ps1
findings.description: Detected what appears to be a Hardcoded secret/password in configuration file.
findings.remediation: Move to a secrets manager or ensure this file is in .gitignore. Never commit real credentials to version control.

findings.title: Potential path traversal in screenshot save path
findings.description: File save path may incorporate user-controlled data (symbol from webhook). A crafted symbol like '../../etc/passwd' could write outside the screenshots dir.
findings.remediation: Sanitize symbol name: symbol = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9]', '', symbol)

findings.title: No rate limiting on API endpoints
findings.description: Found 28 API endpoints with no rate limiting middleware. An attacker can flood /webhook with thousands of requests to exhaust Binance API quota or trigger unwanted trades.
findings.evidence: Endpoints: GET /health, GET /dashboard, GET /, GET /tv_health_check, GET /api/mcp/status
findings.remediation: Add slowapi or custom rate limiter middleware (e.g., 10 req/min on /webhook).

findings.title: Potential path traversal in screenshot save path
findings.description: File save path may incorporate user-controlled data (symbol from webhook). A crafted symbol like '../../etc/passwd' could write outside the screenshots dir.
findings.remediation: Sanitize symbol name: symbol = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9]', '', symbol)

findings.title: Potential path traversal in screenshot save path
findings.description: File save path may incorporate user-controlled data (symbol from webhook). A crafted symbol like '../../etc/passwd' could write outside the screenshots dir.
findings.remediation: Sanitize symbol name: symbol = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9]', '', symbol)

findings.title: Potential path traversal in screenshot save path
findings.description: File save path may incorporate user-controlled data (symbol from webhook). A crafted symbol like '../../etc/passwd' could write outside the screenshots dir.
findings.remediation: Sanitize symbol name: symbol = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9]', '', symbol)

findings.title: Dynamic import — potential code injection
findings.description: Call to __import__() detected. This can execute arbitrary code.
findings.remediation: Remove __import__() or use a safe alternative.

findings.title: DRY_RUN mode overridable via environment variable
findings.description: BINANCE_DRY_RUN is loaded from environment at startup. If .env file is writable or env can be injected, an attacker could disable dry-run mode and execute real trades.
findings.remediation: Add runtime validation: if production, force DRY_RUN=true unless explicit unlock.
